what_it_does: A client-side, face-driven generation ladder (the Bench) that walks a user through seven rungs—Brief, Root, Plan, Titles, Frames, Deck, Keep—to grow a multi-page artifact from a single root line. It reads live sequence, voice, layouts, and grammar faces; derives a page plan and archetype assignments; renders interactive wireframes and a mini deck; persists state in localStorage; and exports a spec via POST or clipboard fallback.
observations: Wrapped in a strict-mode IIFE and depends on globals CTX and window.DNA.
Reads live faces (sequence, voice, layouts, grammar) and derives PLAN client-side from the sequence face.
Persists the growing spec under localStorage key 'dna-bench-spec'.
Renders seven accordion rungs with CSS grid-template-rows transitions.
Builds archetype wireframes as inline SVGs using grammar proportions and layout focal zones.
Deck cards are tinted by the warmth midpoint of their plan page.
The 'Keep' action POSTs to /api/grown and falls back to clipboard or a bench-only message.
Voice invariants are checked live: no hedging words, title length, no trailing period.
Structural changes (tier, arc, n) cascade downstream and re-render later rungs.
Uses string-based HTML generation with a small escaping helper e().
standouts: derivePlan ports server-side sequence resolution into the browser and supports fusion tiers.
wireSVG synthesizes layout-specific SVG wireframes from grammar proportions and focal markers.
The keep action is the only 'gold' action and degrades gracefully when the house API is unavailable.
Deck cards use CTX.org organisms (hubNetwork, mesh, cascade, detailStrip) for role-specific visuals.
Locked rungs enforce a root-first progression with inline gate messages.
conventions: Prefixed helpers ($, e) and small utility functions (clamp, norm, escRe).
State object S is mutated directly and mirrored to localStorage.
HTML is assembled by string concatenation and injected via innerHTML.
Event wiring happens after each body render.
Debounced renderDeck(220 ms) limits re-rendering during typing.
CSS is injected as a single concatenated string at module bottom.
concerns: Relies on CTX and window.DNA globals without visible guards in the shown code.
String HTML construction risks injection if e() or CTX.esc() is incomplete.
localStorage load merges without schema validation.
Plan derivation mutates rung count objects in place.
Re-rendering bodies may leave stale timers or listeners if not carefully managed.
The provided file is truncated, so the full CSS and IIFE close are not visible.
notes: A self-contained, face-driven artifact builder that turns a brief and root line into a derived page plan, wireframe deck, and keepable spec.
questions: What initializes CTX and where do CTX.org.* helpers live?
How are the four faces loaded into FACES?
What does window.DNA.ordinal and CTX.tok() return?
Is /api/grown implemented elsewhere, and what does it expect?
How is this IIFE mounted or invoked by the surrounding page?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file implements the seven-rung ladder, live-face derivation, localStorage persistence, and keep action described in its header comment.
apparent_themes: Root-first growth: every rung is a complete artifact-so-far.
Live derivation from canonical faces rather than invented values.
Warmth, register, and density as page dials.
Offline-first resilience for the keep action.
intention_signals: To make artifact generation feel like tuning an instrument: each rung is a complete valid artifact-so-far, every value is derived from canonical faces, and the user only advances once prerequisites are satisfied.
novelty: The ladder treats each rung as a whole artifact; wireframe SVGs are synthesized from grammar proportions and layout focal zones; deck cards are tinted by the warmth midpoint of their plan page; the keep action degrades gracefully from server save to clipboard to bench-only message.
contribution: Provides an interactive room that materializes the sequence/voice/layouts/grammar faces into a brief-to-deck workflow, including client-side plan derivation, voice-rule checking, archetype wireframe rendering, mini deck generation, and persistence/export plumbing.
summary_for_embedding: bench.js is an interactive client-side generation ladder that builds a multi-page artifact from a brief and root line by deriving a plan from live sequence, voice, layouts, and grammar faces, rendering wireframes and a mini deck, and persisting state to localStorage.
intention_for_embedding: To give users a self-contained Bench for growing a presentation or document root-first through seven rungs and keeping the resulting spec.

symbols.does: Returns the default bench state object.
symbols.description: Creates a fresh spec with step 1, a default brief, an empty root, and empty titles and frames arrays.

symbols.does: Restores state from localStorage.
symbols.description: Parses the 'dna-bench-spec' item and merges it into the blank default, preserving brief defaults where missing.

symbols.does: Writes current state to localStorage.
symbols.description: Serializes the global S object and stores it under 'dna-bench-spec', swallowing errors.

symbols.does: Shorthand for document.getElementById.
symbols.description: A tiny DOM lookup helper used throughout the module to find ladder elements.

symbols.does: Escapes regex metacharacters.
symbols.description: Prefixes special regex characters with backslashes so a string can be safely embedded in a RegExp.

symbols.does: Clamps a number to a range.
symbols.description: Returns v bounded between lo and hi using Math.max and Math.min.

symbols.does: Strips a parenthetical suffix.
symbols.description: Removes trailing ' (anything)' from an archetype name, e.g. turning 'split-bleed (timeline)' into 'split-bleed'.

symbols.does: Returns an ordinal color from the DNA ramp.
symbols.description: Calls window.DNA.ordinal with the page index, total, and CTX.tok() to pick a color for plan ribbons.

symbols.does: Scales a fixed-size SVG into a mini slot.
symbols.description: Wraps an SVG in a scaled-down div so a larger organism drawing fits a small deck card area.

symbols.does: Debounces deck re-rendering.
symbols.description: Clears any pending timer and schedules renderDeck after 220 ms to avoid rendering on every keystroke.

symbols.does: Looks up a voice invariant message.
symbols.description: Searches FACES.voice.invariants for an entry matching the given id and returns its message text.

symbols.does: Checks text against voice rules.
symbols.description: Flags banned hedging words, title word-count outside 3–9, and trailing periods, returning an array of note strings.

symbols.does: Renders voice notes under an input.
symbols.description: Displays the notes from voiceNotes in a holder and toggles a warning class on the input when notes exist.

symbols.does: Computes the dial envelope for a role group.
symbols.description: Aggregates warmth low/high/mid, register, and density from the sequence face narrative_roles for the supplied role keys.

symbols.does: Computes min/max page count for an arc.
symbols.description: Sums the count ranges of each run in the named arc from the sequence face.

symbols.does: Computes allowed page range for the full tier.
symbols.description: Intersects the full tier page range with the current arc range to determine selectable page counts.

symbols.does: Builds the page plan from the sequence face.
symbols.description: Handles fusion tiers by fusing role groups and full tiers by expanding arc runs, assigning affinities, archetypes, and environments to each page.

symbols.does: Trims or pads titles and frames to plan length.
symbols.description: Keeps S.titles and S.frames aligned with PLAN.length, preserving existing entries where they fit.

symbols.does: Returns the selected archetype for a plan page.
symbols.description: Uses the page's affinity list and the stored frame offset to pick the current archetype.

symbols.does: Collapses PLAN into consecutive role runs.
symbols.description: Groups adjacent pages with the same combined roles into runs with key, length, and start index.

symbols.does: Returns position within a run.
symbols.description: Counts backward from page i while roles match to find the page's index inside its run.

symbols.does: Determines whether a rung is accessible.
symbols.description: Returns true for rung 1, requires a subject for rung 2, and requires both subject and root for all later rungs.

symbols.does: Returns a page-count description for a tier.
symbols.description: Produces a human string such as 'the root line alone', a range, or a fusion page count for the given tier key.

symbols.does: Counts non-empty titles.
symbols.description: Returns the number of entries in S.titles that have non-whitespace content.

symbols.does: Produces the summary string shown on a rung header.
symbols.description: Returns a concise status for each of the seven rungs, or an empty string when not yet meaningful.

symbols.does: Looks up grammar proportion parts.
symbols.description: Reads FACES.grammar.proportion.archetypes[name].parts, falling back to the supplied default array.

symbols.does: SVG rectangle helper.
symbols.description: Builds a rect element string with rounded corners, stroke, fill, and opacity for wireframe diagrams.

symbols.does: Draws a small focal marker.
symbols.description: Returns an SVG path shaped like a corner triangle in ochre to mark a focal zone in a wireframe.

symbols.does: Draws a hatched footer rectangle.
symbols.description: Renders a filled rectangle with diagonal lines, used for footer areas in wireframes.

symbols.does: Draws title-area placeholder bars.
symbols.description: Renders a small ochre bar and a larger charcoal bar to represent a title block in a wireframe.

symbols.does: Draws horizontal placeholder stripes.
symbols.description: Renders several rounded rectangles of decreasing width to stand in for text lines.

symbols.does: Generates a wireframe SVG for a layout archetype.
symbols.description: Selects archetype-specific drawing logic for immersive, split-bleed, stack, field, duo, grid, trio, and title-main-footer variants, using grammar proportions and focal zones.

symbols.does: Conditionally renders a focal marker.
symbols.description: Returns a focalCorner only when the layout's focal matches the supplied key.

symbols.does: Returns a warmth-based background tint.
symbols.description: Builds a CSS color-mix between warm and cool using the page warmth midpoint as a percentage.

symbols.does: Builds rotating stat couplets.
symbols.description: Returns an array of four stat pairs rotated by page index so each card highlights different metadata.

symbols.does: Renders stat couplets as HTML.
symbols.description: Formats a subset of statsFor entries into labeled value pairs for deck cards.

symbols.does: Renders placeholder bullet fragments.
symbols.description: Pulls examples from FACES.voice.forms.bullet_claim and emits them as fake bullet lines.

symbols.does: Derives hub node labels.
symbols.description: Uses the first letters of subject words, or the first letters of canonical arc roles, as node labels for a hub network organism.

symbols.does: Chooses an organism visualization by lead role.
symbols.description: Returns a hub network, mesh, couplets, cascade, or detail strip depending on whether the lead role is show, argue, prove, plan, or people.

symbols.does: Renders placeholder text lines.
symbols.description: Builds several divs with decreasing width to represent body copy in a deck card.

symbols.does: Renders an immersive one-pager deck card.
symbols.description: Produces a full-page card for open/close immersive pages with a pool, kick, root line, house icon, and warmth footer.

symbols.does: Renders a plan page deck card.
symbols.description: Builds a card for page i with a kick line, title, archetype-specific main content, and dial footer.

symbols.does: Renders the full deck strip.
symbols.description: Populates the bn-deckstrip element with bookCard or deckCard for each plan page, then updates rung 6 result.

symbols.does: Builds a selectable chip button.
symbols.description: Returns HTML for a button with group/value data attributes, label, optional subtext, and an on state class.

symbols.does: Builds the page-count picker row.
symbols.description: Generates chip buttons for every allowed page count within the current fullRange.

symbols.does: Returns HTML for the Brief rung.
symbols.description: Renders subject, reader, occasion, purpose, tier, arc, and page-count controls, plus notes and the next button.

symbols.does: Returns HTML for the Root rung.
symbols.description: Renders the root line input, voice-note holder, and the generation_ladder stage note for the root stage.

symbols.does: Returns HTML for the Plan rung.
symbols.description: Shows a ribbon of derived pages with roles, warmth, register, and density, or a message when the root is the whole piece.

symbols.does: Returns HTML for the title row preview.
symbols.description: Builds a preview line of page titles, using dashes or the root line when no plan pages exist.

symbols.does: Returns HTML for the Titles rung.
symbols.description: Renders the row preview and grouped title inputs per role run, each with live voice-note feedback.

symbols.does: Returns HTML for a frame card.
symbols.description: Builds a clickable card showing the page number, roles, wireframe SVG, archetype title, and cycling hint.

symbols.does: Returns HTML for the Frames rung.
symbols.description: Renders a grid of frame cards and the button to open the deck.

symbols.does: Returns HTML for the Deck rung.
symbols.description: Renders the deck strip container and the Keep button.

symbols.does: Returns HTML for the Keep rung.
symbols.description: Shows a digest of the piece and the Keep button with its lock note.

symbols.does: Returns HTML for a ladder rung section.
symbols.description: Builds the accordion section markup for rung k, including header button, result span, and body container.

symbols.does: Updates a rung header result.
symbols.description: Sets the result text, locked/done styling, and a brief dim transition when the text changes.

symbols.does: Updates all rung header results.
symbols.description: Calls setRes for rungs 1 through 7 to refresh the ladder summary line.

symbols.does: Updates gate messages and button states.
symbols.description: Shows or hides the subject/root gate messages and disables the next buttons when prerequisites are missing.

symbols.does: Toggles the keep button lock.
symbols.description: Locks the Keep button and shows a note until the brief purpose field is non-empty.

symbols.does: Opens a rung if unlocked.
symbols.description: Sets S.step to k, saves, toggles open classes on rung sections, and updates all result lines.

symbols.does: Renders and wires a rung body.
symbols.description: Injects the body HTML for rung k and calls wireBody to attach event listeners.

symbols.does: Re-renders rungs after a structural change.
symbols.description: Re-renders bodies from rung 3 onward and refreshes results and gates when the plan changes.

symbols.does: Shows the downstream cascade note.
symbols.description: Briefly displays 'the change flows down the ladder' in the Brief rung.

symbols.does: Attaches event listeners to a rung body.
symbols.description: Wires next buttons, brief chips, subject/purpose inputs, root input, title inputs, frame cards, and the keep button.

symbols.does: Cycles a frame card to its next archetype.
symbols.description: Advances the frame offset for the page, re-renders the card with a fade, and updates downstream results.

symbols.does: Attaches interaction to a frame card.
symbols.description: Binds click and Enter/Space keyboard handlers to cycle the card's archetype.

symbols.does: Assembles the keepable spec object.
symbols.description: Collects brief, root, derived plan pages with titles and dials, and the versions of the faces used.

symbols.does: Persists the spec to the house or clipboard.
symbols.description: POSTs the spec to /api/grown; on failure copies JSON to the clipboard or shows a bench-only message, then re-enables the button.

symbols.does: Shows a transient toast message.
symbols.description: Displays a message in bn-toast for about 4.6 seconds, clearing any previous timeout.
